title: More recently, in Maunsell v Olins [[1975] AC 373 at 391F] Lord Simon said 'statutory language must always be given presumptively the most natural and ordinary meaning which is appropriate in the circumstances'.

evidence.clean_text: More recently, in Maunsell v Olins [[1975] AC 373 at 391F] Lord Simon said 'statutory language must always be given presumptively the most natural and ordinary meaning which is appropriate in the circumstances.' Judges of the present day show no inclination to abandon the presumption.
